title: Fanout Exchanges and True Publish/Subscribe · Type Recall
summary: A delayed handoff reconstructs fanout exchanges and true publish/subscribe from bounded publish, route, delivery, acknowledgement, and recovery evidence, challenges one misconception, and revises the fanout subscription topology and lifecycle map. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for fanout subscription topology and lifecycle map after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Give each independent subscriber responsibility its own queue so every responsibility can receive and acknowledge its copy.
Use fanout routing when every binding should match and record the resulting copy count and storage responsibility.
Design exclusive server-named subscriptions for session-scoped state and account for disconnect, binding, declaration, and cleanup races.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Subscriber Queue Per Responsibility · changed case · Review Subscriber Queu…
steps.instruction:
review case — A review messaging team handles case 9001 in a multi-tenant case event backbone with three subscriber responsibilities. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove one subscription queue per responsibility and explicit late-subscriber behavior; separate browser evidence from disposable live-broker proof; then close the channel while one acknowledgement decision is still pending. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Which action demonstrates Subscriber Queue Per Responsibility without dropping earlier contracts?

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Subscriber Queue Per Responsibility · changed case · Review Subscriber Queu….
